mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases real borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months with declining PAYEMS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units, aggregate demand weakness signals reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM AND HOUST < 1400 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 689443 million and HOUST at 1427 thousand as of 2022-11-01
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in > 62% of instances when both RSAFS declines and HOUST < 1400, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1.5% annualized over a 3-month window indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by > 0.5 percentage points month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4.0%, the combination signals Fed pivot toward easing without labor market deterioration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 298.112 and UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2022-12-01
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL deceleration and UNRATE < 4.0% is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter falls below 0.5% annualized rate, signaling economic stagnation and reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of quarters with GDPC1 growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1500 thousand units while real retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, indicating robust consumer demand and housing investment that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500 thousand AND RSAFS grows MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1427 thousand and RSAFS at 689443 million as of 2022-11-01
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between HOUST > 1500 and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
